name: Blackthorn
description: The Blackthorn (Prunus spinosa) is a thorny shrub or small tree deeply embedded in Celtic folklore and tradition. Its association with the darker aspects of the year, particularly winter and the transition between seasons, makes it a powerful and often feared plant. The Blackthorn's sharp thorns are seen as protective, but also dangerous, representing boundaries, challenges, and the need for caution. It is linked to the Otherworld and the realm of spirits, often associated with gateways and thresholds. The tree's dark wood and bitter sloes (fruits) further contribute to its somber reputation. In Celtic tradition, the Blackthorn is connected to magic, divination, and protection. Its branches were sometimes used to create wands or staffs for spellcasting and defense. The sloes were used in preparations for medicinal purposes, and the wood was believed to possess protective qualities. The Blackthorn is a symbol of resilience, transformation, and the cyclical nature of life, death, and rebirth. Its presence in the landscape serves as a reminder of the hidden power and potential dangers that exist in the natural world and the importance of respecting the boundaries between worlds. It is a potent emblem of both defense and adversity, a powerful symbol of the Celtic spirit's ability to endure even the harshest conditions. It represents the strength found in facing challenges head-on and the wisdom gained through overcoming obstacles. The Blackthorn reminds us that beauty and danger can coexist, and that true strength lies in understanding and respecting both.
subtitle: Guardian of Thresholds, Harbinger of Winter

keyMyths.title: Blackthorn and the Winter Queen
keyMyths.description: The Blackthorn is said to be a favorite of the Winter Queen, representing her power and the harshness of winter. Its thorns are her guardians, and its fruit her bitter reward for endurance.

sources.author: Miranda Green
sources.work: Animals and Plants in Celtic Life and Myth
sources.description: Examines the role of flora and fauna in Celtic culture and mythology.
